headline: Sticky ideas: simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, stories
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—SUCCESs on the page:
Sticky ideas: simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, stories—own the stage.
Not more noise—designed to lodge in memory's grip—
Flip it for today, make the message stick.
[Chorus]
Sticky ideas run on SUCCESs—six levers, one aim,
Simple core, unexpected break, concrete claim.
Credible proof, emotional care, story that plays in the mind,
Heath brothers: sticky is designed, not luck you find.
Forget the jargon flood; build for the brain that forgets by noon,
Sticky is the tune that survives the scroll and the room.
Simple, surprise, touch, trust, feel, tale—
SUCCESs—don't fail the scale.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick cracked why some ideas live and most die in the hallway after the meeting,
Urban legends beat corporate vision statements because they hit every feeling and detail seating.
Simple is finding the core, not dumbing down into mush,
Commander intent: one priority when the plan turns to rush.
Unexpected opens a gap—break the schema, then fill it with truth,
Curiosity is the hook of youth and the booth of adult attention proof.
Concrete beats abstract: kidney stones and VELCRO, not "synergize excellence,"
Credible: stats help, but a single vivid test or authority presence can fence doubt.
Emotional: people act when they care for people, not only for pie charts,
Mother Teresa scale—one child moves more hearts.
Stories: flight simulators for the mind—rehearse the act before the act,
SUCCESs is a checklist against the fact that most messages get cracked by forgetfulness.
Lineage of sticky: proverbs, parables, ads that outlive the product cycle,
History of failed launches: smart people, dead messages—no vehicle.
Meat: stickiness is craft you can audit line by line,
Not charisma alone—design time.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: product launch deck—run SUCCESs on slide one before feature soup,
Simple core in twelve words, unexpected proof, one concrete user loop.
TikTok, Reels, LinkedIn carousels—same physics: surprise then teach, face then feel,
AI can draft walls of text; you prune to sticky real.
Pitch to investors: bury the lead and die; lead with the kidney-stone concrete of the pain,
School lessons: one sticky story over twenty abstract aims.
Slack announcement nobody reads? SUCCESs rewrite: one sentence core, one unexpected stat, one story of a teammate,
Parent note to kids: concrete chore + why it cares—debate ends neater.
Make one small finished thing that could only exist if sticky SUCCESs were true in your hands—
A one-page sticky brief for your real project—no fluff stands.
Tomorrow: that finished brief on the desk, not in the cloud of good intentions—
From Made to Stick—mention it, then live the dimensions.
[Bridge]
Brains dump almost everything by Thursday...
Sticky is the Thursday that stays...
Six levers, not six decorations...
Build—then repetition earns its station.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So sticky ideas: simple, unexpected, concrete, credible, emotional, stories—Heath law,
Act tomorrow: one finished sticky object that obeys the draw of SUCCESs raw.
Audit your last message against the six; rewrite until three strangers can retell it tomorrow night,
If they can't, it wasn't sticky—fix it right.
When the 2020s drown truth in content volume and AI sludge,
Be the person whose one idea still lodges.
One brief, six checks, ship the version that can travel without you—
SUCCESs—do.
Simple surprise—concrete trust—heart story.
Make it stick—glory.

headline: Find the core message
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—strip to the bone:
Find the core message—leave the rest alone.
Not everything important can be the lead; choose the commander intent—
Flip it for today, core first, then invent.
[Chorus]
Find the core message—one idea that steers the rest,
If everything's priority, nothing's stressed as best.
Heaths: simplicity is finding the essential, not the shallow,
Core is the north star when details howl and swallow.
Say the core so a stranger could pass it on whole,
Then nest the rest under that pole.
Core clear—clutter dies—
Find it—rise.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick opens hard on compacting: journalists bury leads, companies bury meaning,
Core message is the sentence you'd save if the building were leaning.
Southwest's bags-fly-free era, military commander's intent—examples of core as decision filter,
When plans shatter, core still runs the filter.
Forced prioritization hurts because smart people love their ten points equally,
Sticky work demands a cruel beautiful inequality: one point leads, nine support legally.
Lineage of proverbs: whole cultures compress wisdom to cores that travel generations,
History of failed strategy decks: fifty slides, zero regeneration in the hallway conversations.
Meat: core is not a tagline contest alone—it's operational language for tradeoffs,
What you will not do is how the core coughs its proof off.
Simple core survives translation across teams, apps, and panic,
Complex mush dies in the first traffic of the organic.
So hunt the core with "what's the single most important thing?" then cut until it hurts productively,
That's the Heaths' reductive creativity.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: make one small finished thing that only exists if "find the core message" is true—
A twelve-word core on a sticky for your launch, your lesson, your family rule set new.
AI summary of your doc? Don't accept the bullet salad—force the model to one core, then verify,
Product roadmap: one core outcome this quarter or the board will bury the why.
Startup about page, personal brand bio, school unit plan—same surgery,
Phone-free twenty-five if you need it—core on paper, no posting luxury.
Slack status, App Store subtitle, cold email subject—core or delete,
Parenting: one family core for the week ("kind mouths") beats ten nagging tweets of discipline.
Tomorrow: finished core card taped where you work—if a stranger reads it, they get the project—
Made to Stick—object.
[Bridge]
More points feel like more care...
Often more points are how meaning disappears...
Core is love with a knife...
Cut to the life of the idea.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So find the core message—Heath brothers' first sticky commandment in practice,
Act: one finished core artifact, twelve words max if you can match the application tactic.
Restate, then run a 25-minute block that only serves that core—no side quests, no feed,
Core becomes deed.
When culture rewards longer decks and denser threads,
Be the person who ships the sentence that heads the herd.
Core written, work aligned, tomorrow decisions filtered through it—
Find core—do it.
One line—many doors.
Core first—yours.

headline: Open curiosity gaps
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—crack the schema:
Open curiosity gaps—make them need the answer, not just hear ya.
Unexpected is a gap between what they know and what you show next—
Flip it for today, open the gap, then text the fix.
[Chorus]
Open curiosity gaps—question before the dump of facts,
Mystery is sticky; lectures bounce off backs.
Heaths: break the guessing machine, then fill the hole with truth that fits,
Gap open—mind commits.
Not clickbait lies—honest surprise that earns the fill,
Curiosity is the will to stay still and learn the skill.
Open the gap—close it well—
That's the spell.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick treats unexpected as structure: schemas predict; good messages violate prediction carefully,
Then resolve so the brain pays the fee of attention gladly.
Curiosity gap theory (Loewenstein lineage in the book's logic): we hate incomplete knowing,
So pose the puzzle the audience thought they finished owning.
"What makes a message stick?" lands harder after "smart ideas die every day"—gap then path,
Journalists use delayed leads; teachers use mysteries; sticky makers do the math.
Lineage of sticky ads and science writing: headline as gap, body as bridge,
History of ignored warnings: no gap, no care—facts like sludge.
Meat: open gaps ethically—don't withhold forever for engagement farming alone,
Fill with concrete credible care once the mind is loaned to you.
Too much gap with no fill is fraud; no gap at all is forgettable prose,
Sticky lives in the dose.
Surprise is a tool, not a personality—design the break in expectation,
Then teach into the opening.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: make one small finished thing that only exists if curiosity gaps are true—
A lesson cold-open, a product teaser email, a team memo that starts with "most of us believe X—here's why wrong."
YouTube and TikTok already weaponize gaps; you can weaponize them for real learning,
AI blog posts that start with throat-clear? Rewrite: open a gap first, then earning.
Onboarding: don't dump feature lists—open "why do new users quit on day two?" then answer in product,
Parent homework help: "guess what happens if…" before the formula.
Pitch deck slide one: gap, not logo soup,
Newsletter: subject line as honest mystery, body as payoff—not empty loop.
Tomorrow: one finished gap-open piece for a real audience—measure if they lean in—
Made to Stick—begin.
[Bridge]
Knowing feels finished until you show the crack...
Attention is the tax they pay when schemas lack...
Open, then honor the debt with truth...
Gap—then proof.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So open curiosity gaps—Heath craft for attention that doesn't apologize for being designed,
Act: finish one gap-first message for work, class, or craft—aligned.
No feed post required—just a real human who feels the pull and gets the fill clean,
Curiosity as machine, not meme scene.
When feeds open gaps only to sell rage and nothing to teach,
Be the person whose gap leads to reach of useful speech.
One open, one honest close, sticky tomorrow because they had to know—
Gap—go.
Surprise with honor—teach through the door.
Open—then more.

headline: Use credible details
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—trust is a texture:
Use credible details—proof in the picture, not only the lecture.
Authority helps; vivid accurate particulars help harder—
Flip it for today, detail as armor.
[Chorus]
Use credible details—make belief feel earned, not begged,
A sharp true speck beats a fog of claimed "world-class" eggs.
Heaths: credibility is statistics, authorities, and human-scale proof you can see,
Details that smell like reality.
Not fake precision—honest grit that says "I was there or I measured,"
Sticky trust is treasured when details are weathered and true.
Credible—concrete—kept—
Details—adept.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick shows how urban legends stick partly because of needless-seeming details that signal truthiness,
The exact street, the exact brand—brain treats them as witnesses.
Credible sources: experts, celebrities carefully, anti-authorities who "shouldn't" agree,
But also internal credibility: a message that carries its own verifiable seeds.
Sinatra test, testable credentials, vivid stats that scale to human life (not only billions floating),
Details turn abstract claims into something court-worthy in ordinary fighting for attention.
Lineage of sticky journalism: scene-setting particulars; history of empty corporate speak: zero details, zero trust,
Meat: one accurate sensory or numeric anchor can out-argue ten adjectives of "robust."
Beware false detail for manipulation—Heath ethics ride with effectiveness,
Sticky should serve true ideas, not con the defenseless.
So harvest details from real users, real labs, real nights in the problem,
Put them where doubt usually blooms.
Credibility is designed into the sentence, not only bolted on as a logo row,
Details make the idea go.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "use credible details"—show up even if mood disappears—
Rewrite one pitch, lesson, or about-page with three true particulars that clear the smears of hype.
AI drafts love vague superlatives—your job is inject measured, checkable flesh,
Case study: not "customers love us"—"47 teachers cut prep by 12 minutes on Tuesdays"—mesh with truth.
App store listing, grant application, science explainers for kids—same rule,
Screenshot, timestamp, named constraint—school the cool of empty brand fuel.
Sales call: one customer quote with context beats ten slides of "innovative,"
Parent trust: specific plan ("homework 7:10–7:35 at the table") beats "we'll do better" looping.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, three credible details locked into one real message—
Heaths' edge.
[Bridge]
Hype shouts; detail nods and stays...
Trust is built in the phrase that could be checked on Mondays...
Adjectives fade; particulars hold...
Credibility—told in the bold of the small true.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So use credible details—Made to Stick's trust technology for anyone who ships ideas,
Act: phone-free hour, mood optional, details non-negotiable in the writing.
Three true anchors in one artifact—if someone challenges, you can show the source or the scene,
Sticky and clean.
When AI and ads flood the zone with fluent emptiness,
Be the person whose sentences have bones of evidence.
Hour done, message tougher, belief more available tomorrow—
Details—borrow from reality, not from vanity.
True grit in the line—trust climbs.
Credible—time.

headline: Make people care
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—attention isn't action:
Make people care—emotion is the traction.
Facts inform; feeling moves the feet and the wallet and the vote—
Flip it for today, care is the moat.
[Chorus]
Make people care—associate the idea with something they already love or fear fair,
Self-interest, identity, other-people, or principle—pick a stair.
Heaths: analytical numb is real; sticky messages warm the nerve,
Without care, clever dies in reserve.
Not cheap tear-jerk only—honest stakes a human can hold,
Care turns told into sold into bold action cold mornings.
Make them care—then teach—
Heart then reach.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick hits Maslow wrong-order myths and "people care about people" over mass statistics alone,
Mother Teresa: "If I look at the mass, I will never act"—one face is the known.
Sporting-event effect, identity appeals, appealing to self-interest without shame when true,
Emotional is the E in SUCCESs—without it, S-U-C-C-S is a mute lecture queue.
Lineage of sticky causes: famine photos, personal stories beside policy,
History of ignored climate charts until a home, a kid, a coast feels nearby.
Meat: make care by association—tap existing values; don't invent feeling from zero every time,
Or use the unexpected to jolt then the emotional concrete to climb.
Avoid pure fear without efficacy—care plus a path beats despair paralysis,
Sticky emotion still needs the rest of SUCCESs for lasting thesis.
So design: who is the human in this idea, what do they stand to lose or gain that I can feel in my chest,
Then write from that test.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: restate "make people care" in twelve words max—run one 25-minute work block that obeys—no posting.
Block output: one message rewritten so a real person has a face, a stake, a next step.
Fundraising, product marketing, safety training at work—same fail mode: feature lists, no pulse,
AI can generate empathy theater—you must ground care in true stakes, not false pulse.
Teacher: why this math matters for this kid's world before the algorithm,
Manager: change memo that names what people lose and keep—not only KPI theater.
Health habit apps: care about the future self with a concrete scene, not only streak guilt,
Parent: care-first conversation before correction—relationship is the built.
Tomorrow: twelve-word restate, 25 minutes, one caring rewrite shipped to a real channel private or team—
Made to Stick—beam.
[Bridge]
Smart without care is a museum plaque...
Care without truth is a trap...
Sticky needs both heat and light...
Make them care—then get the idea right.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So make people care—Heath brothers' emotional non-optional for sticky impact,
Act: twelve words, twenty-five minutes, one message with a human contract.
No posting required—care proven when someone not-you feels the stake and moves one inch,
That's the clinch.
When feeds harvest outrage care for clicks and leave people empty,
Be the person who makes care serve a true useful idea gently and firmly.
One rewrite that could make a stranger act tomorrow morning—
Care—warning complete, door opening.
Feel first—then facts land.
Make care—command.

headline: Stories simulate and stick
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—plot as practice room:
Stories simulate and stick—mental rehearsal in the gloom before the real boom.
Not decoration after the data—stories are how brains run the drill—
Flip it for today, story as skill.
[Chorus]
Stories simulate and stick—flight sim for behavior change,
Hear a tale, walk the path, rearrange what you'll arrange.
Heaths: stories carry inspiration and knowledge in one vehicle that travels,
Plot is how sticky unravels into action that settles.
Challenge, connection, creativity plots—templates that move feet,
Story beats a bullet list in the heat of the street.
Simulate—then stick—then do—
Story true.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick closes SUCCESs with Stories as the sixth lever with unique power,
Jared and Subway, medical diagnoses taught as cases—story as trainer hour.
Simulation: listeners encode sequences, causal chains, emotional stakes as if partially lived,
That's why stories outlive slides—they've already driven the mind where you needed.
Springboard stories (Denning lineage adjacent in sticky practice) help orgs change by showing not only telling,
History of proverbs and parables: civilization's sticky USB sticks of telling.
Meat: a good sticky story is simple enough to retell, concrete enough to see, emotional enough to care,
And still true enough to dare trust.
Three useful plots in the book world of stick: challenge (underdog climb), connection (bridge people), creativity (solve novel),
Pick the plot that matches the goal of the novel idea you're selling to the skull.
Stories aren't anti-data—they're the wrapper that lets data survive the trip home,
Without story, home is where ideas go to die alone.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "stories simulate and stick"—show up mood or not—
Write one true short story that teaches your core idea without a feature dump on the spot.
Onboarding: hero's first week as narrative, not PDF maze,
Sales: customer journey story with credible details, not only ROI haze.
TikTok case studies, founder threads, teacher hooks—story is the native format already—
Use it on purpose for simulation, not only for vanity.
AI can spit generic hero journeys; you supply the ugly true middle that makes simulation accurate,
Kids' lessons: story problem before symbolic form—sticky and pragmatic.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, one finished story under 400 words that a teammate could retell—
Heaths' spell.
[Bridge]
Lists inform the clipboard...
Stories train the pilot...
Simulation is respect for how brains actually change...
Plot the range of the action you want arranged.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So stories simulate and stick—Made to Stick's invitation to stop underusing narrative,
Act: phone-free hour, one true teaching story, no feed carnival.
If they can retell it tomorrow and almost feel they lived the choice,
You shipped sticky voice.
When content farms flood with fake stories for engagement,
Be the person whose real story earns the arrangement of behavior.
Hour done, story done, idea more portable than your presence—
Simulate—presence optional, plot essential.
Tell true—train minds.
Story—binds.

headline: Do not bury the lead
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—front the news:
Do not bury the lead—put the core where eyes first choose.
Journalism's sin is sticky teaching's sin: save the point for later, lose the room—
Flip it for today, lead first, then bloom.
[Chorus]
Do not bury the lead—first sentence earns the second,
Hide the point and the point gets beckoned into oblivion.
Heaths: inverted pyramid energy—most important up top, support below,
Lead clear—ideas go further than you know.
Suspense has a place; chronic burying is fear dressed as craft,
Lead loud—then the rest can laugh and attach.
Unbury—lead—stay—
Say it today.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick uses the buried lead as the enemy of sticky communication,
Writers fall in love with setup, context, credentials—audience leaves mid-confession.
Lead is the core message wearing its work clothes in line one or the subject line,
Everything else is optional clothing once the spine is fine.
Lineage of newsrooms: editors cut from the bottom because the top must stand alone,
History of corporate emails: context paragraphs kill the bone of the ask alone.
Meat: burying often comes from anxiety—softening, hedging, refusing to choose the core,
Sticky requires the courage of the lead before the lore.
Unexpected gaps can open first—but the lead of what matters still can't hide three screens deep,
Structure is respect for scarce attention's steep cost of keep.
So draft, then ask: if they only remember one thing, is it already in the first breath?
If not, unbury—or accept the death of the message.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "do not bury the lead"—show up even if mood disappears—
Take three real messages (email, doc intro, landing hero) and force the lead into the first two lines clear.
Slack walls, Notion pages, AI-generated blogs—chronic bury machines—you become the unburier,
App onboarding: value lead before account creation theater.
Teacher slides: learning goal sentence before the biography of the topic,
Manager feedback: the point first, then evidence—not a mystery novel of soft openers.
Cold outreach: lead with their problem solved in one line, not your journey monologue,
Texts to family: the plan up top—logistics love the lead.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, three unburied artifacts ready to send—
Made to Stick—amend.
[Bridge]
Setup feels polite and often is delay...
Lead feels blunt and often is the way...
They can always read deeper if the first hook holds...
Bury nothing gold.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So do not bury the lead—Heath practical journalism for every maker who writes to move,
Act: phone-free hour, unbury three leads, prove the groove.
Mood optional; clarity mandatory; first lines must survive alone on a lock screen,
That's the scene of sticky hygiene.
When longform culture and AI verbosity reward the scenic route to nowhere,
Be the person whose lead is somewhere useful at once.
Three messages fixed—tomorrow's default is lead-first fingers—
Unburied—lingers.
Point first—respect time.
Lead—climb.

headline: First command attention, then hold it
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—two jobs, one flow:
First command attention, then hold it—don't only show, make them go with you.
Hook without hold is a bounce; hold without hook is a lecture empty seats—
Flip it for today, command then keep.
[Chorus]
First command attention, then hold it—surprise opens, sticky fills,
Unexpected gap, then concrete credible emotional skills.
Heaths: attention is rented second by second; design both the grab and the grip,
Command—then ship the trip through the idea complete.
Don't mistake a loud open for a finished job,
Hold with story, core, and details that don't rob the clock.
Open strong—stay strong—
Hold long.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick separates getting noticed from staying understood and remembered,
Unexpected is often the command; SUCCESs full set is the hold when attention is threatened.
Schema violation wakes the brain; simple core and concrete trail keep it from re-sleeping on the next tab,
Emotional and story glue the hold when abstract would scab over.
Lineage of great talks and ads: cold open, then relentless clarity,
History of viral-for-a-second fluff: command without substance—hold fails, brand is a casualty.
Meat: plan the first five seconds and the next five minutes as different design problems,
Same message system, two phases of the problem.
Command can be a question, a vivid wrong assumption, a startling true number,
Hold is the honest fill that rewards the number with meaning, not only another stunner.
Stunner after stunner without core is carnival; core without command is library nobody enters,
Sticky walks both centers.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: each morning for 7 days, read this line from Chip and Dan Heath, then take one matching action in under 2 minutes—
Two minutes: rewrite one subject line or thumbnail text (command), then one supporting first paragraph (hold).
YouTube, Reels, lecture openers, standup demos—train both muscles daily micro,
AI hooks are cheap; your hold must be the expensive truth they keep.
Meeting: first sentence commands why we're here; middle holds with one story and one decision,
Onboarding email: command the outcome, hold with one path and one credible detail.
Kids' attention: command with mystery object, hold with hands-on—not only louder voice,
Notifications: if you command attention, you owe a hold worth the cost of choice.
Tomorrow: day one of seven—two-minute command+hold pair on a real message—
Heath habit—message.
[Bridge]
The open is a knock...
The hold is the talk that makes the knock worth answering...
Both are craft...
Skip neither half.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So first command attention, then hold it—Made to Stick's two-beat rhythm for living messages,
Act: seven mornings, two minutes, command then hold on something that matters in your practice.
By day seven you'll feel the limp opens and the empty holds in your own drafts—fix both,
Sticky growth.
When platforms pay only for the command and abandon the hold to rage or ads,
Be the person who pays the hold tax with useful substance.
Seven micro-reps—then one full piece that does both end to end—
Command—friend to hold—send.
Wake them—walk them home.
Attention—owned.

headline: Craft for memory
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—design the after:
Craft for memory—what remains when the talk is laughter and hallway chatter.
If they can't retell it, it didn't stick—build for the retell test—
Flip it for today, memory as the quest.
[Chorus]
Craft for memory—write what survives the commute home,
Not what dazzled in the room alone.
Heaths: sticky is a memory technology—schema, story, concrete hooks,
Memorable is the books of the mouth when the slides unhook.
Repeatable core, vivid image, emotional tag, story spine—
Craft so the mind can find it next time.
Memory first—ego second—
Craft the reckoned.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick is obsessed with the curse of knowledge: you remember because you know; they don't,
So craft as if memory is scarce storage with brutal competition for the slot.
Simple cores compress for storage; concrete images encode dual (word+picture); stories provide structure,
Unexpected creates the "event" memory tag that future cues can puncture back open.
Lineage of oral culture: rhyme, proverb, parable—memory tech before cloud sync,
History of forgotten training: beautiful decks, zero behavioral link after a week.
Meat: the retell test is the product test—can a non-expert pass your idea to another non-expert intact?
If not, you crafted for performance, not for memory's contract.
Generation effect, spacing, cues—Heaths practical cousins to learning science without the jargon flood,
Your job is portable memory goods.
So strip, concretize, story-bind, and leave a handle (phrase, image, name) people can grab later,
That's craft, not prayer.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: restate "craft for memory" in twelve words max—run one 25-minute work block that obeys—no posting.
Block goal: one message reduced to a retellable kernel plus one image or story handle.
Employee handbook section, investor update, lesson plan, feature announce—run the retell test on a friend,
AI verbosity is anti-memory—your craft is the compression friend.
Spaced follow-up: sticky phrase in day-one and day-three pings without spam theater,
Flashcards for teams? Fine—if the card is the core, not trivia.
Parent rules: one memorable family phrase beats a speech every night,
Brand: memory is the real SEO in human networks.
Tomorrow: twelve words, 25 minutes, kernel + handle finished—ask someone to retell after an hour—
Made to Stick power.
[Bridge]
Applause is not recall...
Recall is the real curtain call...
Craft for the Tuesday after...
Memory is what you're after.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So craft for memory—Heath standard that humbles pretty writing without a residue,
Act: twelve-word restate, twenty-five minutes, retell-test your residue.
If they fumble the core, rewrite until a tired person at 9 p.m. can still pass it on,
That's the win condition.
When content metrics count views not residual memory in behavior,
Be the person who optimizes for what remains after the session's over.
Kernel sharp, handle sticky, retell clean—tomorrow act on the remembered—
Craft—surrendered to the brain's real rules.
Stay in mind—drive the deed.
Memory—lead.

headline: Message design is creative work
song:
Yo, Chip Heath and Dan Heath, Made to Stick—stop treating comms as afterthought:
Message design is creative work—same craft rank as the product you bought thought.
Ideas don't spread themselves; someone designs the sticky shape—
Flip it for today, design the escape of the idea into heads.
[Chorus]
Message design is creative work—not admin, not leftover slides,
SUCCESs is a studio practice, not a vibe that "just rides."
Heaths: respect the art and science of making ideas portable,
Creative sweat on the message is responsible, not optional.
Write, test, cut, concretize, storyboard the care—
Message craft is R&D for share.
Design the saying—own the staying—
Creative—paying the cost of clarity.
[Verse 1]
Made to Stick reframes communication from "decorate the truth" to "engineer memorability and force,"
Curse of knowledge makes experts bad natural designers—they need deliberate craft, of course.
Creative work means generating options: multiple cores, multiple stories, multiple unexpected angles,
Then selecting with SUCCESs as rubric, not only with "I like this" tangles.
Lineage: ad creatives, teachers, preachers, founders who wrote the napkin line that built the company,
History of great products with dead messaging—and weak products with sticky mythology.
Meat: schedule time for message design like you schedule build time; staff it; iterate it with outsiders,
Don't assign it to the last hour before launch with exhausted writers.
Creative constraints help: six SUCCESs checks, retell test, lead-first, one core—
Constraints are the floor of the design gym, not a bore.
So honor writers, teachers, PMs who shape language as makers, not "soft" support,
Sticky culture promotes that sport.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "message design is creative work"—show up even if mood disappears—
Treat the hour like a studio: three draft cores, two stories, one unexpected open, pick winners with SUCCESs.
Product teams: pair a designer of pixels with a designer of phrases in the same sprint,
AI is a junior copy intern—you are creative director of truth, not a paste sprinter.
Launch week: protect message design hours from only bug bashes,
Classroom: lesson hook design is creative work—prep it like a lab, not a cab ride afterthought.
Personal brand, nonprofit appeal, safety campaign—same studio ethics,
Side project: the landing sentence is product.
Tomorrow: phone-free studio hour, finished message package (core+hook+story+detail)—
Heaths' wage for the page.
[Bridge]
Build without message is a tree falling unheard...
Message without craft is a bird with one wing absurd...
Creative work on words is how ideas inherit the earth of attention...
Design—mention it in the budget of your intention.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So message design is creative work—Made to Stick's meta-lesson over all ten insights,
Act: phone-free hour as studio, not as chore of "comms."
Leave with a package only creative sweat could produce—core, gap, details, care, story, lead unburied,
SUCCESs married.
When orgs treat language as leftover and AI as autopilot,
Be the person who puts message craft on the critical path pilot.
One hour, one designed message, ready to test on a human tomorrow—
Creative—borrow the seriousness of engineering for the sentence.
Craft the say—ship the stay.
Message—make.

headline: STEPPS drive contagion: social currency, triggers, emotion, public, practical, stories
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—why things catch:
STEPPS drive contagion: social currency, triggers, emotion, public, practical, stories—that's the map.
Not random virality—designed principles for word of mouth—
Flip it for today, STEPPS out loud.
[Chorus]
STEPPS drive contagion—six reasons ideas travel far,
Social currency, triggers, emotion—raise the bar.
Public visibility, practical value, stories that carry the payload,
Berger: contagion is code you can load.
Stop praying for viral; build the physics of share,
STEPPS is the pair of science and care for what spreads where.
Currency, cue, feeling, seen, useful, tale—
STEPPS—don't fail.
[Verse 1]
Contagious bottled research on what people talk about and pass along,
Social currency: we share what makes us look smart, in-the-know, or strong.
Triggers: environment cues that keep the idea top of mind after the campaign dies,
Emotion: high-arousal feelings (awe, anger, anxiety, humor) fuel the tries of sharing more than mild.
Public: if behavior is visible, it advertises itself (apple logo, Livestrong era logic),
Practical value: news you can use gets forwarded because helping is social glue.
Stories: Trojan horses—idea inside a narrative people actually want to tell,
STEPPS is a checklist when you launch, teach, fundraise, or sell.
Lineage of diffusion research meets marketing storytelling; history of "went viral" luck myths get demystified,
Meat: diagnose which STEPPS you have and which you're missing before you spend.
Not every product needs all six equally—but zero of six is a hope strategy,
Design contagion like strategy.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: name one habit that fights STEPPS thinking—maybe "post and pray" with no trigger plan—
Cut that habit in half this week while you practice Contagious on a real thing in hand.
Audit your side project, nonprofit drive, or team ritual against S-T-E-P-P-S on one page,
TikTok growth, Slack emoji culture, open-source stars—same six-stage.
AI can brainstorm STEPPS angles; you pick honest ones that fit the product truth,
School fundraiser: social currency for kids, public thermometer, practical tip sheet, story of one student—proof.
Product hunt launch without practical value or story? Add them before boost spend,
Triggers: link your idea to a daily cue (coffee, commute, Monday standup) so talk doesn't end.
Tomorrow: half the pray-posting, one finished STEPPS one-pager for something you actually ship—
Berger's grip.
[Bridge]
Viral isn't magic weather...
It's psychology people share together...
Six levers, infinite mixes...
STEPPS fixes "why didn't it spread?" guesses.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So STEPPS drive contagion—Berger's full stack for talk that moves ideas through networks,
Act: cut the anti-habit half, finish the STEPPS map, pick two levers to strengthen before next share.
When vanity metrics chase views without word-of-mouth design,
Be the person who builds why people tell two friends on purpose, not by accident.
One page, six boxes filled honestly, one experiment this week—
Contagious—speak.
Currency cue emotion public practical story—
STEPPS—glory of designed spread.
Map it—run it—watch talk travel.
STEPPS—level.

headline: Design for talk-worthiness
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—make it remark-able:
Design for talk-worthiness—give people a reason to remark, full table.
If nobody has a line worth saying at dinner, your idea dies quiet—
Flip it for today, design the diet of conversation.
[Chorus]
Design for talk-worthiness—shareable because it's worth the breath,
Not only good—good to say out loud before the death of the thread.
Berger: social currency lives here—we talk to look sharp and connected,
Talk-worthy is selected by ego and delight inspected.
Build the remarkable detail, the rule-break, the insider tip,
Design the quip they can grip.
Worth saying—worth spreading—
Talk-ready—heading out.
[Verse 1]
Contagious opens hard on word of mouth as more persuasive than ads, and on designing for it,
Talk-worthiness is the product quality of "I have to tell you about…" lit.
Remarkable means worthy of remark—Snapple facts energy, secret menus, unexpected policies,
Not only quality; quality that generates a sentence in someone else's mouth easily.
Social currency: sharing the find makes the sharer look like a curator of cool or savvy,
Mimic that honestly: exclusivity, inner rings, performance of knowledge carefully.
Lineage of "water cooler" hits and modern group chats—same hunger to contribute value and status,
History of great products nobody mentioned—and gimmicks everyone did for a week of gratis PR.
Meat: ask "what would someone actually say?" Write that sentence; if you can't, redesign,
Talk-worthiness is a spec, not a hope line.
Break expected scripts a little—safe enough to share, surprising enough to need airtime,
That's the design climb.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: restate "design for talk-worthiness" in twelve words max—run one 25-minute work block that obeys—no posting.
Block output: one feature, policy, lesson, or story angle engineered so a stranger has a clear remark.
Restaurant specials, SaaS Easter eggs, classroom demos, conference booths—same job,
AI feature lists are not talk-worthy—one shocking true outcome might be the blob that works.
Group chat culture: craft the update your teammate wants to forward to another channel,
Creator economy: the clip is the remark—design the line they duet.
Nonprofit: one startling efficient fact as social currency for donors at brunch,
Parent community: one tip so good it migrates across WhatsApp without you.
Tomorrow: twelve words, 25 minutes, one talk-worthy element finished in the real work—
Contagious—quirk with purpose.
[Bridge]
Good can be silent...
Talk-worthy is good with a handle on it...
Give the mouth a job...
Design the blob of a sentence that does the marketing slog.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So design for talk-worthiness—Berger's first practical filter before budget for ads,
Act: twelve-word restate, twenty-five minutes, one remark engineered in the labs of your craft.
Test it: can a friend say your line without notes and look good saying it?
If not, iterate the hit.
When teams ship "solid" and wonder why silence follows,
Be the person who ships a talkable edge that still tells truth, not hollow.
One remark designed—tomorrow listen for it in the wild—
Talk-worthy—styled.
Make it sayable—make it said.
Design—spread.

headline: Environment triggers recall
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—top of mind is designed:
Environment triggers recall—cues in the wild keep you aligned.
Triggers link your idea to everyday stimuli so talk recurs—
Flip it for today, plant the spurs.
[Chorus]
Environment triggers recall—coffee, commute, calendar, kit,
Frequent cues make frequent talk if the link is fit.
Berger: Rebecca Black Friday jokes, kit kat and breaks—triggers as free media,
Link tight—idea stickier, needier of less constant feed-ya.
Don't only launch loud; linger via cues in the habitat,
Triggers are the habitat ad that never sleeps flat.
Cue—recall—remark—
Trigger—spark.
[Verse 1]
Contagious explains why some campaigns die after the Super Bowl and others hum for months,
Triggers are environmental reminders that re-activate the concept in ordinary haunts.
More frequent the cue, more frequent the thought—if you've built the association strong,
Mars bars and Mars rovers curiosities—adjacent science of what primes the song of mention.
Lineage of classical conditioning meets marketing; history of products tied to seasons, times, tools,
Meat: pick a trigger that already happens often in your audience's life—then bind honestly.
Weak triggers: rare events; strong triggers: daily objects, weekly rituals, common complaints,
Competitors may own a cue—find an open one or out-associate with a cleaner claim.
Triggers also explain why bad news or jokes reappear on anniversaries—calendar as cue machine,
Design for the machine, not only the day-one scene.
So map customer day: where could your idea naturally come to mind? Build the link in message and product,
That's Contagious product.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: each morning for 7 days, read this line from Jonah Berger, then take one matching action in under 2 minutes—
Two minutes: name one environmental cue for your project and one sentence that binds idea to cue.
Habit apps already use triggers; your launch can too—Monday standup = quality reminder, not only metrics,
Coffee break content for kit-kat logic: pair your tip with a real daily act.
Classroom: hang the cue poster where the behavior should fire; product: in-email tied to payday or tax season,
Health: tie the habit to an existing brush-teeth anchor—Berger meets clear behavior design.
Brand: sonic logos and UI chimes are triggers—use ethically, not only addictively,
Side project: pick "every time I open Slack" as cue for a micro-practice you sell to yourself.
Tomorrow: day one—two minutes, cue chosen, link sentence written on a sticky near the cue—
Contagious—do.
[Bridge]
Attention fades; environments remain...
Triggers borrow the remain to re-explain...
Associate with the frequent...
Recall becomes convenient.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So environment triggers recall—Berger's ongoing free distribution system for ideas,
Act: seven mornings, two minutes, strengthen one cue-link until it's automatic in your brain first.
Then ship that trigger in customer language so their world does the reminding,
Not only your ad spending.
When teams buy more ads instead of better triggers,
Be the person who maps the day and plants the idea where life already occurs.
Seven micro-binds—one public trigger live in a real message by week's end—
Recall—friend.
Cue the world—keep the word.
Trigger—heard.

headline: High-arousal emotion spreads
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—feeling has voltage:
High-arousal emotion spreads—awe, anger, anxiety, amusement—wattage.
Low-arousal sadness can stall shares; activation makes the fingers move—
Flip it for today, feel to prove the groove of spread.
[Chorus]
High-arousal emotion spreads—pulse up, pass on,
Content that flatlines feelings dies by dawn.
Berger: physiological arousal fuels sharing more than mild contentment alone,
Light the bone of emotion—don't only intone the known.
Awe elevates, anger activates, humor spikes, anxiety alerts—
Design the volts—alert the networks.
Feel hard—share more—truth still required—
Arousal—wired.
[Verse 1]
Contagious digs into NYT most-emailed research patterns: awe-inspiring science travels,
Anger and anxiety can too; pure sadness often less—arousal is the clue of physiological ready-to-act.
Exercise and even caffeine analogies in the research story—activation state matters when the ask to share arrives,
Emotion is not one blob; high vs low arousal splits the archives of what thrives.
Lineage of pathos in rhetoric meets modern share buttons; history of PSA fails that only depress without activate,
Meat: choose the emotional register that fits your truth—don't fake rage for reach if awe or humor fits better ethically.
Practical: open with a moment that raises heart rate slightly—wonder, injustice named cleanly, laugh of recognition—
Then deliver practical or story payload while the system is on.
High arousal without credibility becomes ragebait trash; Berger's science is descriptive and designable with care,
Sticky Heath cousins: emotion that makes people care—here tuned to share.
So audit your draft: what do they feel in the body by line three?
If nothing, redesign.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "high-arousal emotion spreads"—show up even if mood disappears—
Rewrite one real message to raise honest arousal: awe at a result, clean anger at a fixable wrong, humor that reveals.
Newsletter, launch tweet thread, all-hands memo, classroom hook—same voltage check,
AI default tone is polite flatline—you add the human spike that isn't fake wreck.
Nonprofit: one story that awes at resilience better than a guilt slog that freezes,
Safety training: anxiety with a clear action path—arousal plus efficacy, not panic cosplay.
Product: delight spike in the first run (onboarding surprise) so users tell with raised pulse,
Politics and feeds weaponize this—your job is high-arousal for useful true pulse.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, one finished high-arousal true piece ready to send to a real audience—
Contagious—use it.
[Bridge]
Mild is forgettable in the feed war...
Voltage is how ideas leave the drawer...
Arouse with honor—then guide the energy...
Spread—tenderly or urgently, but not emptily.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So high-arousal emotion spreads—Berger's physiological key for word-of-mouth ignition,
Act: phone-free hour, mood optional, voltage non-optional in the composition.
Feel it yourself when you read it back—if your body doesn't move, theirs won't share,
Iterate until the air changes in the chair.
When platforms reward any arousal including toxic,
Be the person who picks high-arousal that still serves truth and constructive aims.
One charged true message—shipped—tracked for talk not only likes—
Arousal—likes become lives when people speak.
Raise the pulse—pass the useful.
Emotion—fuel it.

headline: Make behavior public when helpful
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—visible is viral's cousin:
Make behavior public when helpful—let the act advertise the dozen who follow.
Private choices don't teach the room; public ones social-proof the move—
Flip it for today, show to prove, carefully choose.
[Chorus]
Make behavior public when helpful—design observability in,
If they can't see it, they can't copy the win.
Berger: public is the P that turns users into walking billboards without paid boards,
Visible votes, logos, lines, badges, queues—cords of social cords.
Not all privacy should die—choose when helpful means better norms and safer starts,
Public by design—smart arts.
See it—mirror it—spread it—
Public—credit the visible habit.
[Verse 1]
Contagious argues ideas and products catch when the behavior leaves a trace others can observe,
Apple's glowing logo in a dark room era, Livestrong bracelets, Movember—observe and absorb.
Social influence needs information: what are people like me doing?
Private good deeds and private product use fail to self-advertise the boon.
Lineage of social proof research; history of silent majorities that never shape norms because silence hides the majority,
Meat: build public artifacts of private value—shareable outputs, default-visible choices that aren't shaming.
Queues signal quality; open metrics can; uniforms and stickers can; "powered by" can,
Design the residue of use as marketing without making users feel used as only ads.
When helpful: health norms, voting (with care), learning streaks that inspire not crush, tipping culture visibility debates—
Judgment required—public can also pressure wrong.
Berger's lean: if you want diffusion of a good behavior, hide it less.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: restate "make behavior public when helpful" in twelve words max—run one 25-minute work block that obeys—no posting for vanity—build a visibility feature or ritual.
Examples: open roadmap votes, classroom work-on-walls, team demo Fridays, donation thermometers, GitHub public green only if healthy,
Product: export that creates a beautiful public artifact users are proud to show.
Privacy-first apps: still can make optional public modes for those who want the contagion benefit,
School: showcase process not only grades—public learning without humiliation design.
Habit: gym check-in culture, reading lists shared in family chat—choose helpful public,
Anti-pattern: forced public shame rankings—that's not Berger's helpful lane.
Tomorrow: twelve words, 25 minutes, one finished visibility mechanism in your real project—
Contagious—project it.
[Bridge]
Hidden good is still good...
Hidden good is slow to neighborhood...
Show the behavior you want multiplied...
Public is how norms get certified.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So make behavior public when helpful—Berger's social-proof engineering for ethical spread,
Act: twelve-word restate, twenty-five minutes, ship one observe-and-copy affordance.
If a newcomer can't see the desired behavior, they can't join it—fix the see,
Then celebrate without coercive glee.
When products dark-pattern public for vanity metrics only,
Be the person who makes public serve learning, trust, and true quality signals.
One visibility ship—watch copycats of the good kind appear—
Public—clear.
Show the act—grow the pact.
Visible—fact.

headline: Practical value gets shared
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—useful is a social act:
Practical value gets shared—we pass what helps, and that's a fact.
News you can use is currency of care in networks—
Flip it for today, pack the useful so it flecks outward.
[Chorus]
Practical value gets shared—tips, deals, how-tos, saves,
Helping others helps us look good and feels brave and kind in waves.
Berger: utility travels because sharing is prosocial and smart-looking,
Useful content is the booking for word-of-mouth without cooking fake drama.
Cut the fluff; leave the rule of thumb they can use at 5 p.m.,
Practical—gem.
Helpful—forwarded—trusted—
Value—musted into the message.
[Verse 1]
Contagious highlights all the deal-sharing, life-hack, health-tip, money-save transmission,
Practical value is the STEPPS P that powers kitchen-table and group-chat permission to spam-good.
People share to help—true motive—and also gain social currency as the helpful node,
Design both: real utility + easy package for the node to load into a chat.
Lineage of consumer reports and grandmother advice columns; history of viral listicles that actually saved time or cash,
Meat: specificity beats vague inspiration—"save 20% if you…" beats "be wiser with money" mash.
Diminishing: too much false urgency or scammy "practical" destroys trust permanently,
True practical value compounds reputation for the sharer and the source.
Package: checklists, thresholds, if-then rules, one-screen recipes, templates—
Make the useful portable.
So ask: what can they do differently tomorrow because of this? If nothing, it won't get the share of helpers.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: each morning for 7 days, read this line from Jonah Berger, then take one matching action in under 2 minutes—
Two minutes: write one concrete tip from your domain someone could use today; save it for a real channel later.
Stack seven tips into one "practical pack" by week's end—newsletter, team wiki, parent handout, product changelog with use-cases,
AI can draft fluff how-tos—you verify the tip works in your hands first.
Customer support FAQs turned into shareable cards; teacher cheat-sheets kids actually stick on lockers,
Fintech, health, cooking, code snippets—practical is the native tongue of forward.
Anti-pattern: engagement bait posing as tips—detect and delete,
True: one threshold rule ("if X, do Y") that survives contact with reality.
Tomorrow: day one tip written, tested once if possible—
Contagious—simple.
[Bridge]
Inspiration without instruction stalls...
Instruction without truth harms...
Practical value is love with a manual...
Shareable—annual trust in every channel.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So practical value gets shared—Berger's reminder that usefulness is a growth engine,
Act: seven mornings, two minutes, one real tip each—build the pack, then release the engine.
When content strategy chases emotion only and forgets the save-this utility,
Be the person whose audience forwards you because life got easier.
Seven tips, one curated shareable artifact, one grateful stranger who didn't need a hard sell—
Practical—well.
Help them—watch them help you spread.
Useful—thread.

headline: Stories carry ideas
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—Trojan horse narrative:
Stories carry ideas—hide the payload in a tale people want to live.
They don't share bullet points; they share plots that make them look alive—
Flip it for today, story-drive the idea inside.
[Chorus]
Stories carry ideas—narrative is the vehicle, lesson is the freight,
Strip the story and the idea may wait at the gate of forget.
Berger: people pass along tales; embedded info rides free,
Design the story so the moral is the key they can't drop accidentally.
Not story then idea as appendix—idea woven so retelling keeps it,
Carry—don't leave it.
Plot on—lesson in—spread thin? No—spread far—
Story—jar of the idea.
[Verse 1]
Contagious shows Trojan horse stories: the narrative is entertaining enough to travel,
While brand, lesson, or product detail is built into the bones so it can't be unraveled without killing the tale.
Jared-type lineages (also Heath sticky cousins) prove story+proof; Berger stresses shareability of the vessel,
If the story is good but the idea is detachable, people may tell a version that drops your essential.
Lineage of fables, case studies, founder lore, customer saga; history of ads people quote without the brand—failure mode,
Meat: build necessary details into the plot (the brand as the tool that saved the day, the rule as the twist).
Stories also provide social currency and emotion and practical value sometimes all at once—STEPPS stack inside narrative,
That's efficient craft.
So draft the story people want to tell at dinner; stress-test: after three retellings, is your idea still in the room?
If not, reweave the loom.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "stories carry ideas"—show up even if mood disappears—
Write one short true story where removing your core idea would break the plot logic.
Case study page, sales leave-behind, lesson opener, nonprofit appeal—same weave test,
AI loves generic hero journeys—you supply the ugly specific middle that carries the real thesis.
Product: a user story where the feature is the only reason the ending works,
Team values: a story of a hard call that only makes sense if the value is believed.
TikTok: 30-second story with the tip inseparable from the punchline,
Parents: family story that carries a safety rule better than a lecture.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, finished Trojan story under a page, retell-tested on one person—
Contagious—done.
[Bridge]
Facts need a ride...
Stories provide the ride people choose...
Embed so the passenger can't be left at the curb...
Narrative—verb for the word you want heard.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So stories carry ideas—Berger's narrative rule for contagion that lasts past the click,
Act: phone-free hour, one woven tale, idea locked in the brick of the plot.
If they can tell it without your lesson, you built entertainment not a carrier—fix the weave,
Until the idea never leaves.
When brands bolt logos onto unrelated memes,
Be the person whose story dies if the idea is removed—integrity of design.
One story, one inseparable payload, one mouth that passes both—
Carry—true.
Tale moves—lesson stays.
Story—ways.

headline: Word of mouth can be designed
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—kill the luck myth:
Word of mouth can be designed—not only prayed for with a pitch.
Psychology has levers; campaigns can pull them on purpose—
Flip it for today, design the surface where talk happens.
[Chorus]
Word of mouth can be designed—STEPPS as the toolkit,
Stop treating chatter as weather you can't fit.
Berger: research-backed principles turn "hope it spreads" into "build why they'd tell,"
Design the shell of conversation as well as the product itself.
Talk is not pure chaos; it's patterned human motives,
Design for motives—share velocity.
Intentional—measurable—iterable—
WOM—literal craft.
[Verse 1]
Contagious' big claim is agency: creators, marketers, teachers, founders can shape what gets discussed,
By engineering social currency, triggers, emotion, publicness, practical value, stories—discussed above, now as meta.
Word of mouth is more trusted than ads; designing it is not manipulative by default—it's clarity about human nature,
Ethics live in whether the talk is earned by real value or only by tricks of stature.
Lineage of diffusion of innovations meets behavioral design; history of "it just went viral" post-hoc stories that hide the design that was there—or the luck that won't repeat.
Meat: put WOM design on the roadmap—workshops with STEPPS, pre-mortems for silence, experiments on which lever moves your segment.
Measure mentions, referrals, unsolicited quotes, not only impressions,
Iterate like product.
So the principle is empowerment with responsibility: you can design talk; you should design talk worth having.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "word of mouth can be designed"—show up even if mood disappears—
Build a one-page WOM plan for a real launch: which STEPPS, which sentence people say, which trigger, which public cue.
Startups, teachers launching units, open-source maintainers, local businesses—same plan shape,
AI can draft the plan; you stress-test with three humans: "would you actually say this?"
Referral programs that only bribe without talk-worthiness still flop—design both incentive and remark,
Community managers: seed stories and practical tips, not only announcements.
Internal culture: design word of mouth for a new process so it spreads without mandate spam alone,
Personal projects: ask what would make a friend text another friend—build that.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, finished WOM design doc with one experiment scheduled—
Contagious—mode.
[Bridge]
Luck is real and rare...
Design is repeatable care...
Talk follows motives you can map...
Stop the nap of "maybe it'll catch."
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So word of mouth can be designed—Berger's anti-fatalism for anyone who needs ideas to move,
Act: phone-free hour, WOM plan, one experiment with a date to prove.
When teams outsource all spread to paid ads and influencers only,
Be the person who designs why ordinary people talk between the buys.
Plan done, experiment live this week, learn which lever actually moves your crowd—
Design—proud.
Talk by design—trust by delivery.
WOM—every.

headline: Product features can be marketing
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—build the ad into the thing:
Product features can be marketing—shape the wings so use spreads the ring.
Not only campaign after ship—features that create talk, public cues, practical wins—
Flip it for today, market from within.
[Chorus]
Product features can be marketing—design the share into the use,
Every interaction a chance to choose contagion cues.
Berger: the best marketing sometimes is a feature that makes behavior public or remarkable,
Product is the vehicle.
Stop bolting ads on silent tools; make the tool announce value as it works,
Features—perks that speak in networks.
Built-in—spoken—shown—
Product—own the known.
[Verse 1]
Contagious blurs product and promotion: a distinctive design, a visible logo, a shareable output, a default that signals,
Hotmail's early invite trails, referral mechanics, unboxable moments—product as campaign missiles.
If the experience generates social currency ("look what I found"), you've marketed by building,
If it leaves public residue, you've advertised by existing.
Lineage of growth loops and design-as-marketing; history of brilliant campaigns for products that still don't give people anything to say from use alone.
Meat: in roadmap reviews, ask which features increase STEPPS—not only which increase retention in silent solitude.
A progress bar shared, a certificate, a collaborative artifact, a surprising quality detail users photograph—
Those are marketing features.
Ethics: dark patterns that force spam are not the lesson; helpful visible value is.
So PM and marketer sit together at spec time, not only at launch week panic.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: book one phone-free hour titled "product features can be marketing"—show up even if mood disappears—
Spec one feature whose primary job is talk, trigger, public proof, or practical share—not only internal metrics.
Consumer app: beautiful export; B2B: public customer story wall opt-in; hardware: a detail people point at;
AI product: a surprising quality moment users screenshot with pride, not only a model card.
Classroom "product": student work that becomes hallway gallery—feature as marketing for the program,
Service business: a deliverable template clients show their boss—your brand rides along.
Tomorrow: phone-free hour, one feature spec or prototype note that only makes sense if this insight is true—
Contagious—through.
[Bridge]
Ads shout from outside...
Features whisper from inside the use and get believed...
Build the whisper loud enough to travel...
Product-market fit includes product-talk fit unravel.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So product features can be marketing—Berger's integration of build and spread,
Act: phone-free hour, one feature designed as a mouthpiece of value earned.
Ship path scheduled; vanity sharebait rejected; real STEPPS in the mechanics—
That's Contagious ethics.
When orgs separate product and marketing into non-talking silos,
Be the person who writes the feature that markets while it works for users.
Spec done—build next—talk measured—
Features—treasured as media.
Build the ad—into the act.
Product—attract.

headline: Science of why things catch on
song:
Yo, Jonah Berger, Contagious—replace myth with method:
Science of why things catch on—study the spread, don't only dread or fetish the luck.
Virality has psychology, social dynamics, designable structure—
Flip it for today, science your culture of launches.
[Chorus]
Science of why things catch on—patterns over superstition,
STEPPS, networks, timing, arousal—conditions of ignition.
Berger: research can explain the hit and improve the miss,
Catch-on is a system you can learn, test, and assist.
Not a crystal ball—better than a shrug and a budget burn,
Science—turn toward what the data of talk actually earns.
Why it spreads—know it—show it—
Catch-on—grow it with method.
[Verse 1]
Contagious stands on studies of what people share, email, mention, adopt—
From online content to offline products, the book argues for general principles of the catch.
Science means hypotheses: if we increase public visibility, does adoption rise?
If we add a daily trigger, does conversation persist past the prize of launch day?
Lineage of social science in marketing; history of folk theories ("sex sells always," "just get influencers") that sometimes fail without STEPPS fit.
Meat: treat every campaign as a chance to learn which principle moved your audience,
Write down predictions; compare to what people actually said in the wild.
The science is also humility: context matters, culture matters, but levers still transfer better than pure chance stories.
So become a student of catch-on in your niche—save examples, reverse-engineer STEPPS, build a swipe file of designed contagion,
That's mastery beyond one book read once.
[Chorus]
[Verse 2]
Modern day apply it: make one small finished thing that could only exist if "science of why things catch on" were true in your hands—
A mini case study: pick three things that spread in your world this month; score them on STEPPS; extract one rule for your next ship.
Or a simple experiment design doc: one lever, one metric of talk, one week window,
AI can summarize Berger; you run the field science on your actual network.
Team lunch-and-learn with evidence from your product analytics and qualitative quotes,
Creator: A/B the emotional arousal or practical tip packaging—log results like a lab.
School project: students reverse-engineer a viral PSA with STEPPS—science literacy meets media,
Local business: track which Instagram post earned unsolicited mentions offline—and why.
Tomorrow: finished case study or experiment sheet—proof you practice the science, not only quote it—
Contagious—fit.
[Bridge]
Myths are comforting and expensive...
Science is humbling and cumulative...
Catch-on leaves clues in every hit and flop...
Collect the clues—don't only chase the top.
[Verse 3 / Outro]
So science of why things catch on—Berger's closing stance for this Contagious set: curious, rigorous, practical,
Act: make the small finished scientific object—case study or experiment—tactile.
From Contagious: one rule you'll obey on the next launch because evidence said so,
Not because a guru's vibe said go.
When culture worships overnight virality without autopsy,
Be the person who runs the autopsy and upgrades the next body of work.
Finished analysis tonight or tomorrow morning—then one designed change in the next ship—
Science—grip.
Know the why—build the try.
Catch on—apply.
